reasoning:
Muqtada al-Sadr recently (April 18, 2025) strongly reaffirmed his intention to boycott the November 11, 2025 parliamentary elections, explicitly stating he "will never participate alongside the corrupt." This was a rejection of a direct public appeal by Iraqi President Abdul Latif Rashid. 

While Sadr frequently uses boycotts as a tactical maneuver and often reverses them (as he did ahead of the October 2021 elections), the timeline makes a reversal before July 1 unlikely. The 2025 elections are scheduled for November 11. In 2021, Sadr waited until late August—about six weeks before the election—to dramatically announce his return. If he follows a similar pattern of leveraging his absence for maximum political concessions and dramatic effect, he is more likely to wait until late summer or autumn (August or September) to make a comeback announcement. Although official IHEC registration deadlines may surface before July, Iraqi electoral politics routinely extend deadlines for major factions, so Sadr will not be rigidly forced to make a decision by July 1. Considering his recent firm repudiation and his penchant for last-minute maneuvers, the probability of him formally announcing his participation before the beginning of July is relatively low.
